method: git diff c5ae389..f8cf298; git worktree checkout of f8cf298 at /tmp/.../mv-resume-hero-review; pnpm install; vitest run (isolated + full suite); tsc --noEmit; next lint; test-transplant of resume-hero-identity.test.tsx onto c5ae389's page.tsx to confirm genuine pre-fix failure; git grep across full apps/web tree.

per_check.note:
git grep -n "VIKRAM|DESHPANDE|Senior Technical Program Manager" f8cf298 -- apps/web/src/app/dashboard/resume/ returns EMPTY (exit 1). Both hardcoded

lines (former page.tsx:219 and :230 on c5ae389) are removed and replaced with conditional identity/empty-state blocks. Whole-tree grep (apps/web/src/) shows only two pre-existing, unrelated hits: apps/web/src/__tests__/dashboard/resume-conversion-tooltip.test.tsx:44 and resume-mv-honesty.test.tsx:25, both a JOB fixture's `location: "Melbourne, AU"` field (unrelated to person identity, present unchanged on c5ae389 too — confirmed via git grep on c5ae389). No other hardcoded fake identity found anywhere in apps/web.

deriveIdentity() (page.tsx new lines ~43-73) reads resume.sections (already in the `resumes` state populated by fetchResumes()) — same object the bullets/label/version rendering already consumes. Priority: contact.name/contact.title → first non-blank line of sections.raw_text → resume.label → "—". No fabricated fallback identity at any tier — worst case falls through to real stored text or an em-dash placeholder glyph, never a person's name/title that didn't come from the resume. VERIFIED backend-side (apps/api/app/services/resume_parser.py:_extract_contact, apps/api/app/routers/resumes.py create_resume/upload_resume) that `contact` never contains name/title in current ingestion paths, so in practice the raw_text-first-line branch is the one that fires in production — this is a real, tested fallback (see test 2 below), not dead/aspirational code. NOTE (non-blocking): the raw_text-first-line heuristic assumes a resume's first non-blank extracted line is the candidate's name, which holds for typical single-column resume layouts but could show a different real line (e.g. a header artifact) for atypical PDF layouts — this is a heuristic-quality caveat, not a placeholder/fabrication violation, since it is still always real, stored user data.

Empty-state copy exact: "No base resume yet." (data-testid=hero-original-empty) and "No tailored version yet." (data-testid=hero-tailored-empty), rendered only when deriveIdentity() returns null (i.e., no resume object at all). Confirmed no invented `location` field: apps/web/src/lib/api/resumes.ts ResumeSchema has no location field, and backend ContactInfo (resume_parser.py) exposes only email/phone/linkedin/github — deriveIdentity()'s ResumeIdentity type is strictly {name, title}, matching the finding's explicit instruction not to invent a location field.

Diff isolated to two additions: (a) new deriveIdentity()/ResumeIdentity block (page.tsx L43-73) and its two call sites (originalIdentity/tailoredIdentity, ~L193-196), (b) the two hero-pane JSX blocks. Format Integrity Check strip (baseHash/formatIntact, page.tsx L157-158 and its render ~L252-268), the version list, diff-vs-parent, and approval-preview sections are byte-identical to c5ae389 (outside the touched diff hunks, per `git diff c5ae389..f8cf298 --stat` = 2 files, +214/-16, both files new-test + page.tsx hero-pane hunks only). tsc --noEmit: 0 errors. next lint --dir src --dir __tests__: 0 warnings/errors.

Transplanted apps/web/src/__tests__/dashboard/resume-hero-identity.test.tsx onto c5ae389's page.tsx (git checkout c5ae389 -- apps/web/src/app/dashboard/resume/page.tsx inside an isolated worktree) and ran `npx vitest run` on that file: all 3 tests FAILED (findByTestId(hero-original-name/hero-original-empty/hero-tailored-empty) timed out — pre-fix DOM has none of these testids, only the literal 'VIKRAM DESHPANDE' text). Restored f8cf298's page.tsx and re-ran the same file: all 3 tests PASS. Not tautological — genuine fail-before/pass-after. Test 1 exercises the contact.name path (Jordan Avery), test 2 exercises the raw_text-first-line fallback (Priya Natarajan) confirming the fallback branch is real and tested (not aspirational dead code), test 3 exercises the fully-empty honest-empty-state path. Each asserts the hardcoded string is absent from document.body.textContent.

Diff is minimal (2 files, +214/-16, no scope creep beyond the finding). No prohibited patterns found: git diff grep for any\b|@ts-ignore|eslint-disable|Math\.random|TODO|FIXME|--no-verify returned empty. `git show --stat f8cf298 | grep -i node_modules` returned empty (no tracked node_modules). `git log c5ae389..f8cf298` shows a single commit, no evidence of --no-verify (commit message is descriptive, not suspicious).

Isolated run: `npx vitest run src/__tests__/dashboard/resume-hero-identity.test.tsx` → 3 passed (3). Full suite: `npx vitest run` → 66 files passed (66), 450 tests passed (450), 0 failures. (Note: `pnpm test -- --run <file>` did not apply the file filter as expected — vitest CLI directly was used instead to get a scoped run; the full-suite run covers the same file plus everything else and is unambiguous.)

non_blocking_observations: deriveIdentity()'s highest-priority branch (sections.contact.name/title) is currently unreachable in production because no backend ingestion path (resume_parser._extract_contact, POST /resumes, POST /resumes/upload) ever populates contact.name/contact.title — the code correctly falls back to the raw_text first-line heuristic, which IS exercised in production and IS covered by test 2. This is a forward-compatible design choice (if a future ingest path adds structured contact.name, it just works), not a defect; flagging only so a future reviewer doesn't assume the contact.name path is load-bearing today.
tests: 3 pass (isolated resume-hero-identity.test.tsx); 450 pass / 450 total (full vitest suite, 66 files); tsc --noEmit clean; next lint clean
